title: Coupon code format templates
description: Every valid code template + substitution rules + entropy characteristics + typical use case. CodeGenerator::generate() reads this catalogue to resolve the code template used by POST /coupons/bulk-issue + admin manual create.

{RAND8}.description: 8-character random Crockford Base32 (24-Z excluding I/L/O/U). ~40 bits entropy.

{RAND4}.description: 4-character random Crockford Base32. ~20 bits entropy — use for short-cycle campaign codes only.

{RAND16}.description: 16-character random Crockford Base32. ~80 bits entropy — use for high-value opaque codes.

{SEQ}.description: Sequential integer, zero-padded to fit count width (SEQ=001 for count<=999, SEQ=0001 for count<=9999, etc.).
{SEQ}.collision_rate_per_tenant: 0 — sequential is guaranteed unique within a bulk-issue call

{CAMPAIGN}.description: Kebab-cased uppercase version of campaign_name (SUMMER-2026 -> SUMMER2026). Refuses when campaign_name is unset.

{TENANT_SLUG}.description: Uppercase kebab-cased tenant slug. Rarely useful — most coupons don't reveal the tenant identity in the code.

templates.name: Random 8-char
templates.typical_use_case: Opaque single-use codes distributed via email or SMS. High entropy — hard to guess.

templates.name: Campaign-only
templates.typical_use_case: Public campaign code — shared broadly. Requires high usage_cap OR unlimited. NOT recommended for high-value discounts (easy to enumerate/share).

templates.name: Campaign + random
templates.typical_use_case: Per-recipient unique codes for a named campaign. Combines campaign attribution with per-recipient uniqueness — the marketing lane can group by campaign_name while the code enforces single-use.

templates.name: Campaign + sequence
templates.typical_use_case: Sequential campaign codes for controlled distribution (physical mailings, printed vouchers). Predictable — do NOT use for online-only campaigns where enumeration is a concern.

templates.name: Referral coupon
templates.typical_use_case: Reserved for referrals-issued coupons (IssueCouponOnReferralRewardVested hook). The 'REF_' prefix identifies the coupon as referral-derived at a glance; issuance_source='referral' on the row is the authoritative discriminator.

templates.name: Random 16-char (high value)
templates.typical_use_case: High-value single-use codes (e.g. $500 credit for a corporate partner). Effectively-unique + resistant to enumeration.

notes: Crockford Base32 alphabet is used because it excludes visually-ambiguous characters (I / L / O / U). Codes are readable + typeable + look-alike-resistant.
The collision_retry_max config (default 5) governs how many times CodeGenerator retries on a unique-index collision. With {RAND8} entropy on per-tenant scope, retries are rare.
For high-value codes ({RAND16}) the retry is effectively never triggered — collision probability approaches 0.

Sequential codes ({SEQ}) do NOT retry because they are guaranteed unique within a bulk-issue transaction. Sequential collisions across bulk-issue calls are impossible because the sequence resets per call.
